member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.ECABehaviour
member.summary: ECABehaviour serves as a foundational component required for all behavior implementations within the automation framework. While only one instance of is attached to a GameObject, it enables and supports specific behaviors such as Toggle or Switch, which inherit from this class and define unique functionality. This class does not contain any specific functionality, but rather serves as a base class for all behavior implementations.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject
member.summary: ECAObject is the base class for all virtual objects that can be used in the automations. All the other classes in this package inherit from this class or one of its subclasses. It supports properties such as position, rotation, scale, visibility, and activity, and provides methods for moving, rotating, scaling, and controlling visibility.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.gameCollider
member.summary: GameCollider is the collider of the object.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.gameRenderer
member.summary: GameRender is the renderer of the object.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.isBusyMoving
member.summary: isBusyMoving is a boolean that indicates if the object is moving.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.description
member.summary: description describes in a few words what the object is and its role.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.p
member.summary: p represents the position of the virtual object in the 3D space. It's a vector with three components: x, y, and z.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.r
member.summary: r represents the rotation of the object in the 3D space. It's a vector with three components: x, y, and z (euler angles).

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.s
member.summary: r represents the scale of the object in the 3D space.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.isVisible
member.summary: visible indicates whether the object is visible. The allowed values are either 'yes' or 'no'. If invisible, the object is not rendered but remains interactive for collisions.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.isActive
member.summary: active indicates whether the object is active. The allowed values are either 'yes' or 'no'. When inactive, the object is not rendered and does not interact with other objects.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.isInsideCamera
member.summary: isInsideCamera indicates whether the object is currently within the camera's field of view. This property is automatically updated at runtime.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Moves(ECARules4All_DLL.Position)
member.summary: Moves (to) is a method that moves the object to a specified position in the 3D space.
member.param: The target position to move to.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Moves(ECARules4All_DLL.Path)
member.summary: Moves (on) is a method that moves the object along a specified path, following sequential points.
member.param: An array of positions the object will follow, one after another.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Rotates(ECARules4All_DLL.Rotation)
member.summary: Rotates sets the object's rotation to a specified value in the 3D space.
member.param: The target rotation expressed as a vector with three components: x, y, and z.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Looks(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Looks adjusts the object's rotation to face a specified target object.
member.param: The target GameObject to look at.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Scales(ECARules4All_DLL.Scale)
member.summary: Scales sets the object's scale to a specified value.
member.param: The new scale value fo the object. The scale is a vector with three components: x, y, and z.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.MovesOriginalPosition
member.summary: Restores the object's original position, rotation, and scale to their initial values.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Shows
member.summary: Shows maakes the object visible if it is not already.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Hides
member.summary: Hides makes the object invisible if it is not already.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Activates
member.summary: Activates makes the object both interactable and visible.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.Deactivates
member.summary: Deactivates makes the object invisible and non-interactable.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.ShowsHides(ECARules4All_DLL.Utils.ECABoolean)
member.summary: ShowsHides changes the visibility state of the object based on a parameter. The parameter can be either 'yes' or 'no'.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.ECAObject.ActivatesDeactivates(ECARules4All_DLL.Utils.ECABoolean)
member.summary: ActivatesDeactivates changes the active state of the object based on a parameter. The parameter can be either 'yes' or 'no'.

Rule describes a single rule, consisting of a "when" clause, a "then" list of actions and the optional "if" condition statements.
See also: ,

: The "then" list of actions(Type: List <>)

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Rule.GetActions

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Condition
member.summary:
Condition is the superclass used by the class for defining checks for rule execution
NOTE: Use for simple statements and for lists of Conditions

members.member:
Evaluate returns the result of the evaluation of the statements contained inside of it.
Returns: A boolean containing the result of the evaluation
if you attempt to instantiate a Condition class it'll always throw this exception, since it's implemented in their subclasses.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Condition.IsLeaf
member.summary:
IsLeaf defines if this node is a leaf or not.
Returns: A boolean that defines if this node is a leaf or not
member.exception: if you attempt to instantiate a Condition class it'll always throw this exception, since it's implemented in their subclasses.

member.summary:

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECADustBall
member.summary: The EcaDustBall component represents the presence of dust on a virtual object, typically a surface equipped with an see component. Dust can be swept away using specialized objects such as brooms, rags, or scottex equipped with an () component. After being swept, the object updates its state through the `allSwept` property.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECADustBall.allSwept
member.summary: allSwept indicates whether all the dust has been successfully removed from the object.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECADustBall._IncreasinglySweeps(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECAScottex)
member.summary: removes-dust-with-scottex defines the sweeping action performed by an object equipped with an component. When the action is executed, the dust balls are removed. Typically, this is the result of a wipes event performed by an object equipped with an .
member.param: The object equipped with an ECAScottex component responsible for performing the sweeping action.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECADustBall._IncreasinglySweeps(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECABroom)
member.summary: removes-dust-with-broom defines the sweeping action performed by an object that has an ECABroom component. When the action is executed, the dust balls are removed. Typically, this is the result of a sweeps event performed by an object that has an component. After removal, nearby objects equipped with an ECADustPan component may automatically execute their collects-dust action to gather the detached dust ball.
member.param: The object that has a ECABroomcomponent responsible for performing the sweeping action.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECAPhysicalGrabbable
member.summary: ECAPhysicalGrabbable is a component that represents a physical object in the scene which can be grabbed by a player, or user, object equipped with an component, using one or both hands. It tracks the grabbing state, manages interaction logic based on trigger collisions with hand colliders, and communicates grab-related events through the automation system.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECAPhysicalGrabbable.grabbed
member.summary: grabbed indicates whether the object is currently being held by a player or user object equipped with an ECACharacter component. This state is updated based on trigger collisions with hand colliders and is used to control interactive behaviors.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECAPhysicalGrabbable._StartsGrabbing(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: starts-grabbing is triggered when a player or user object equipped with an component begins to grab this object with either hand. When triggered, it sets the state to true and notifies the automation system of the grab event.
member.param: The object equipped with an component that initiates the grab action.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECAPhysicalGrabbable._StopsGrabbing(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: stops-grabbing is triggered when a player or user object equipped with an component releases this object with both hands. When triggered, it sets the state to false and notifies the automation system that the grab interaction has ended.
member.param: The object equipped with an component that releases the object.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound
member.summary: Sound is a behavior component that acts as a media player specifically designed for audio playback. It provides functionalities such as playing, pausing, and stopping audio, as well as controlling volume and managing audio sources.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.source
member.summary: Source is the audio filename that serves as the source for playback.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.volume
member.summary: Volume is the current volume level of the audio. Accepts values between 0 and the maximum volume, defined by .

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.maxVolume
member.summary: MaxVolume is the maximum volume level the audio can reach.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.currentTime
member.summary: currentTime is the current playback position in seconds. Tracks the progression of the audio clip.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.playing
member.summary: playing indicates whether the audio is currently playing. The value is either 'yes' or 'no'. If paused or stopped are 'yes', playing will be 'no'.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.paused
member.summary: paused indicates whether the audio playback is paused. The value is either 'yes' or 'no'. When playing again, the audio will resume from the paused time.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.stopped
member.summary: Stopped indicates whether the audio playback is stopped. The value is either 'yes' or 'no'. When playing again, the audio will start from the beginning.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound._audioSource
member.summary: AudioSource is the audio _audioSource that will be used to play the audio.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.sourcePath
member.summary: SourcePath is the path of the audio file.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.Plays
member.summary: Plays starts the audio playback. Updates the state variables playing, stopped, and paused to reflect that playback is active.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.Pauses
member.summary: Pauses pauses the audio playback. Maintains the current playback time (currentTime) for resuming later (by calling Plays).

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.Stops
member.summary: Stops stops the audio playback and resets the playback time (currentTime) to the beginning.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.ChangesVolume(System.Single)
member.summary:
ChangesVolume changes the volume of the audio to a given value.
Ensures the value remains within the range of 0 to

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECASound.ChangesSource(System.String)
member.summary:
ChangesSource changes the audio filename source to the given filename.
Validates the path and dynamically loads the audio for playback.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer
member.summary: Represents a time-based that helps triggering actions after specified durations. The Timer class provides functionality to configure, start, pause, stop, and reset a timer, as well as to emit events when specific time milestones are reached or elapsed.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.duration
member.summary: Duration specifies the total duration for which the timer will run.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.current
member.summary: Current represents the current time of the timer, meaning the elapsed time from the start. It dynamically updates as the timer counts down.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.active
member.summary: Active is a boolean that indicates whether the timer is currently running.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.elapsed
member.summary: Elapsed counts the time elapsed since the timer was started.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.ChangeDuration(System.Single)
member.summary: ChangesDuration sets the total duration of the timer with a non-negative value.
member.param: The new duration value for the timer.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.ChangeCurrentTime(System.Single)
member.summary: ChangeCurrentTime sets the elapsed time of the timer. It ensures that the new value is within the valid range [0, duration].

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.Starts
member.summary: Starts activates the timer to begin counting down, resuming its operation from the last paused state.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.Stops
member.summary: Stops deactivates the timer, resetting the elapsed time to zero.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.Pauses
member.summary: Pauses deactivates the timer, leaving the elapsed time unchanged.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.Reaches(System.Int32)
member.summary: Reaches emits an event when the timer reaches a specified elapsed time. It can be used to trigger actions at predefined points in the elapsed timeline.
member.param: The elapsed time at which the event is triggered.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.Timer.Resets
member.summary: Resets resets the timer to its maximum duration and deactivates it.

member.summary: ECAAnimal is a component that represents an animal character within the ECA rules framework. It extends to provide animal-specific traits and behaviors, enabling actions and interactions characteristic of animal entities.

member.summary: Speaks allows the animal to produce a sound or "speak" by playing an associated audio clip. The audio clip is identified by the provided string, which must correspond to a valid resource.

member.summary: The AquaticAnimal class represents an aquatic animal. An AquaticAnimal can swim to specific positions or follow predefined paths, with animations for both swimming and idling states. This class extends the functionality of to include aquatic-specific behaviors.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.Animals.Subcategories.Mannequin
member.summary: The Mannequin class provides a way to include a character in the scene to wear 3D models of clothes that do not have rigging skeletons. Since the mannequin is supposed to stay still in the environment, the implementation contains for automatically positioning it on top of the mannequin according to the specified position (e.g., head, torso, left or right leg, arm, etc.). Provided that the distinction between a mannequin and a not-playable human is technical, it is up to the Unity developer to decide which object offers the best configuration options considering the template under development.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter
member.summary: ECACharacter is a behavior component that represents a versatile entity within the ECA rules framework. It can embody different forms such as animals, humanoids, robots, or other autonomous or player-controlled beings. The component supports a wide range of actions and state attributes, enabling dynamic interaction with the environment.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter.life
member.summary: life is the current life of the character, represented as a float number.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter.playing
member.summary: playing indicates whether the character is controlled by the player ('yes') or operating autonomously ('no').

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.ECAEnvironment
member.summary: ECAEnvironment is a component that represents non-interactable elements of the environment. Unlike props (), environment items cannot be directly manipulated or interacted with by objects equipped with an component.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.Artwork
member.summary: Artwork represents an artwork in the environment. The Artwork class defines properties such as the author, price, and creation year of the artwork.

member.summary: price represents the monetary value of the artwork.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.Artwork.year

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface
member.summary: ECASurface is a component that represents a virtual surface within the automation environment. It defines surfaces such as tables, floors, walls, or ceilings that can be detected and interacted with by cleaning items () or other objects.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface.type
member.summary: type specifies the kind of surface represented by an object equipped with the ECASurface component. Valid values include 'table', 'floor', 'wall', and 'ceiling'. This attribute is used by cleaning items to determine how to interact with the surface.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.ECAInteraction
member.summary: Interaction represents entities in the scene that facilitate interaction with other objects or the environment. Unlike Behaviours, which define object-based rules and logic, Interaction focuses on physical entities that are perceived as independent objects by the user. These entities exist as standalone components within the environment, enhancing user engagement and interaction.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECAButton
member.summary: Button is an subclass that represents a button. When a is pressed, it will trigger an event defined by the End User Developer.

POV is the camera's point of view.

member.summary:
ZoomsIn reduces the camera's zoom level by the specified amount.
If the resulting zoom is less than 30 the zoom is set to 30.
member.param: The amount of zoom to remove

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECACamera.ZoomsOut(System.Single)
member.summary:
ZoomsOut increases the camera's zoom level by the specified amount.
If the resulting zoom is greater than 100 the zoom is set to 100.
member.param: The amount of zoom to add

member.summary: ChangesPov changes the camera's point of view.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight
member.summary: ECALight represents a controllable light source in the environment. The ECALight class extends to manage light properties such as intensity, color, and if it's on.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.intensity
member.summary: intensity represents the brightness level of the light source. It cannot exceed the maximum intensity value.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.maxIntensity
member.summary: maxIntensity specifies the upper limit for the light's brightness. It ensures that the light's intensity does not exceed a predefined threshold.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.color
member.summary: color represents the color of the light source. The value is a string that represents the color name (e.g., 'red', 'blue', 'green').

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.on
member.summary: on indicates whether the light source is currently active or inactive. The accepted values are 'on' or 'off'.

member.name: F:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.componentLight
member.summary: ComponentLight is the GameObject's light component.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.Turns(ECARules4All_DLL.Utils.ECABoolean)
member.summary: Turns toggles the light source on or off based on the specified value ("on" or "off"), enabling or disabling illumination.
member.param: The desired state of the light source (on or off).

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.IncreasesIntensity(System.Single)
member.summary: IncreasesIntensity increases the brightness of the light source by a specified non-negative amount. If the resulting intensity exceeds the maximum allowed value, it is capped at maxIntensity.
member.param: The value to add to the current intensity.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.DecreasesIntensity(System.Single)
member.summary: DecreasesIntensity reduces the brightness of the light source by a specified non-negative amount. If the resulting intensity drops below zero, it is set to zero to avoid negative values.
member.param: The value to subtract from the current intensity.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECALight.ChangesColor(ECARules4All_DLL.Utils.ECAColor)
member.summary: SetsColor updates the light's color to the specified value. The allowed values are predefined color names (e.g., "red", "blue", "green").
member.param: The desired color to apply to the light source.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Interactions.Subcategories.ECAVideo.ChangesVolume(System.Single)
member.summary: ChangesVolume changes the video volume to the given value. If the value is greater than the max volume, the volume is set to the max volume. If the value is lower than 0, the volume is set to 0.

member.summary: ChangesSource changes the video source to the given value. The new path must be relative to the user-accessible Inventory folder.
member.param: The path for the new video file.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.ECAProp
member.summary: In Prop category we represent generic objects that can be placed in a scene and manipulated by characters. The possible sub-categories are, in this case, several; we can have passive actions.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.ECAProp.price
member.summary: Price: The price of the prop object.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.ECACleaningItem
member.summary: ECACleaningItem is a base component that represents a generic cleaning tool. It provides shared properties and behaviors for all cleaning-related objects and enables integration with other components and ECA-based automation rules.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECABroom
member.summary: ECABroom is a component that represents broom objects within the environment. It defines the properties and capabilities associated with virtual brooms used in automation scenarios.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECABroom.Sweeps(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface)
member.summary:
sweeps represents a triggerable interaction performed by a broom on an object equipped with an component. When sweeps is used as a trigger in an ECA automation, it implicitly defines a causal action within the same automation.
Specifically, if the swept surface is equipped with both and ECADustBall components, the trigger sweeps MUST result in the execution of the removes-dust-with-broom action. This action removes all dust balls present on the surface.
Therefore, in an ECA rule, sweeps should be interpreted not only as a trigger condition but also as a semantic indicator that the automation includes the action removes-dust-with-broom applied to the same surface involved in the trigger.
Optionally, the removal of dust can enable secondary actions (e.g., dust collection) by nearby objects equipped with an component.
member.param: The object equipped with an component representing the surface being swept.
member.summary: sweeps represents the action of a broom cleaning an object equipped with an component, typically a surface within the environment. This method is usually invoked when the broom comes into contact with an object equipped with an component. When sweeps is used as a trigger in an ECA automation, if it interacts with objects equipped with both and components, it MUST trigger in the same automation the corresponding removes-dust-with-broom action. In addition to removing dust, this action generally activates the collects-dust action of nearby objects equipped with an component, allowing them to collect the detached dust balls.
member.param: The object equipped with an component representing the surface to be swept.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECACleaningRag
member.summary: ECACleaningRag is a component that represents a cleaning rag object used to wash objects equipped with an component.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECACleaningRag.Washes(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface)
member.summary: washes simulates the cleaning action performed by a rag on an object equipped with an component, typically a surface within the environment. When executed, it removes dirt, dust, or stains from the surface. If the rag contains water or detergent, the action represents a washing process; otherwise, it performs a dry wiping action. Both cases trigger the washes automation event. When interacting with surfaces equipped with an component, it removes oil stains, and when the surface includes an component, it removes dust balls. This method is automatically invoked when the rag comes into contact with an object equipped with an component, typically detected through a collision event.
member.param: The object equipped with an component representing the surface to be cleaned.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECADustPan
member.summary: ECADustPan is a component that represents a virtual dustpan used in cleaning tasks within the environment. It interacts with objects equipped with an component and allows the collection and containment of dust balls that have been previously removed or swept by other cleaning tools.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECADustPan.CollectsDust(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Behaviours.Subcategories.ECADustBall)
member.summary: collects-dust simulates the action of a dustpan collecting a dust ball from an object equipped with the component, generally a surface within the environment. This method is typically triggered after a sweeps action performed by an object equipped with an component, often in combination with the remove-dust action of an component. When executed, it transfers the dust ball into the dustpan, updating its collected state
member.param: The object being collected by the dustpan.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECAMop
member.summary: ECAMop is a component that represents a virtual mop used to wash objects equipped with an component, typically representing surfaces within the environment.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECAMop.Washes(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface)
member.summary: washes specifies the action of a mop cleaning an object equipped with an component, typically representing a surface within the environment. This method is typically invoked when the mop comes into contact with an object equipped with an component, and it assumes that the mop has the property hasWater set to true. When washes is used as a trigger in an ECA automation, if it interacts with objects equipped with both and components, it MUST trigger in the same automation the corresponding removes-stain-with-mop action (that removes dirt, liquid residues, or stains from the surface as part of the cleaning process).
member.param: The object equipped with an component representing the surface to be cleaned.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECAScottex
member.summary: ECAScottex represents a virtual disposable paper towel used to clean an object equipped with an component, typically a surface within the environment.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECAScottex.Sweeps(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Environments.Subcategories.ECASurface)
member.summary: sweeps represents the action of a paper towel (or scottex) cleaning an object equipped with an component, typically representing a surface within the environment. This method is usually invoked when the paper towel comes into contact with an object equipped with an component. When sweeps is used as a trigger in an ECA automation, if it interacts with objects equipped with both and components, it MUST trigger in the same automation the corresponding removes-dust-with-scottex action (that removes the dust from the surface).
member.param: The object equipped with an component representing the surface to be swept.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem
member.summary: ECASoakableCleaningItem is a component that represents a reusable virtual cleaning item capable of absorbing and releasing various types of liquids, such as water, degreaser, battery killer, or disinfectant. It can be wetted or dried and maintains its current state through dedicated ECA boolean variables, which indicate the presence or absence of specific absorbed substances.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.hasWater
member.summary: hasWater indicates whether the item currently contains water.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.hasDegreaser
member.summary: hasDegreaser indicates whether the item currently contains degreaser.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.hasBatteryKiller
member.summary: hasBatteryKiller indicates whether the item currently contains battery killer solution.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.hasAmuchina
member.summary: hasAmuchina indicates whether the item currently contains Amuchina (a disinfectant).

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem._Wets(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.ECALiquidDispenser)
member.summary: wets represents the action in which an object equipped with the component, such as a cloth, rag, or paper towel, becomes wet after being poured on by an object equipped with the component, such as a bottle or sprayer within the environment. When the is wetted, this event acts as a trigger within an ECA automation. The resulting state change depends on the liquid dispensed: - When the contains water, the cleaning item implicitly performs the action changes-has-water. - When the contains degreaser, it implicitly performs the action changes-has-dDegreaser. - When the contains amuchina, it implicitly performs the action changes-has-amuchina. Executing this action updates the internal state of the cleaning item to reflect the absorbed liquid.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.Dries
member.summary: Dries is an action method that resets the item to a dry state, both visually and logically by clearing the water state variable.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.changesHasWater
member.summary: changes has water represents the implicit action performed when an object equipped with an component absorbs water due to the wets action triggered by an object equipped with a component whose liquidSpawner property is explicitly set to water (e.g., a spruzzino object). In other words, this action is only applicable if the liquid dispenser is configured to dispense water; liquid dispensers configured with any other liquid do not trigger this state change. When this condition is satisfied, the action updates the internal state of the cleaning item by setting the hasWater variable to true, indicating that the object has absorbed water and is now ready for water-based cleaning operations.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.changesHasDegreaser
member.summary: changes-has-degreaser represents the implicit action performed when an object equipped with an component absorbs degreaser due to the wets action triggered by an object equipped with a component whose liquidSpawner property is explicitly set to degreaser (e.g., spruzzinosgrassatore object). In other words, this action is only applicable if the liquid dispenser is configured to dispense degreaser; liquid dispensers configured with any other liquid do not trigger this state change. When this condition is satisfied, the action updates the internal state of the cleaning item by setting the hasDegreaser variable to true, indicating that the object has absorbed degreaser and is therefore ready for degreasing operations.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.CleaningItems.Subcategories.ECASoakableCleaningItem.changesHasAmuchina
member.summary: changes-has-amuchina represents the implicit action performed when an object equipped with an component absorbs amuchina due to the wets action triggered by an object equipped with a component whose liquidSpawner property is explicitly set to amuchina (e.g., spruzzinoamuchina object). In other words, this action is only applicable if the liquid dispenser is configured to dispense amuchina; liquid dispensers configured with any other liquid do not trigger this state change. When this condition is satisfied, the action updates the internal state of the cleaning item by setting the hasAmuchina variable to true, indicating that the object has absorbed amuchina and is therefore ready for sanitizing operations.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.ECAWindow
member.summary: ECAWindow is a component that represents a virtual window within the environment. It can be used as part of automation rules involving environmental interactions, such as opening or closing actions.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.ECAWindow.isOpen
member.summary: isOpen is a boolean state variable that indicates whether a window is open. Its value is automatically updated when the window executes the opens or closes action. This variable can be used as a condition within automation rules related to environmental control.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.ECAWindow.Opens
member.summary: opens represents the action of opening a window. When executed, if the window is currently closed (isOpen = false), it automatically updates the internal state variable isOpen to true and rotates the window to its predefined open position. Executing this action may be involved in automations related to ventilation, lighting, or other environmental interactions that depend on the window’s open state.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.ECAWindow.Closes
member.summary: closes represents the action of closing a window. When executed, if the window is currently open (isOpen = true), it automatically updates the internal state variable isOpen to false and rotates the window to its predefined closed position. This action may be involed in automations related to ventilation, insulation, energy efficiency, or security.

member.summary: batteryKillerDrops counts how many battery killer drops have been added to the container.

member.summary: amuchinaDrops counts how many amuchina drops have been added to the container.

member.summary: temperature represents the current temperature of the liquid mixture inside the container. It is updated dynamically as new liquid drops with different temperatures are added.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidContainer.ECALiquidContainer._FillsIn(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.ECALiquidDispenser)

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidContainer.Subcategories.ECABucket
member.summary: ECABucket is a component that represents a virtual bucket capable of containing different types of virtual liquids within the environment. It extends the functionality of by specializing it as a bucket-type container. The bucket can be filled with liquids such as water, degreaser, disinfectant, or battery killer, and provides visual feedback to indicate both the current fill level and the type of liquid contained.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.ECALiquidDispenser
member.summary: ECALiquidDispenser is a component that represents a virtual dispenser capable of releasing or filling containers with specific types of liquids within the environment. It allows the definition of the liquid type it dispenses, such as water, degreaser, disinfectant, or battery killer, and integrates with other ECA components to support automated filling and wetting actions.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.ECALiquidDispenser.liquidType
member.summary: liquidType specifies the type of liquid dispensed by this object. Possible values include 'water', 'degreaser', 'amuchina', and 'battery killer'.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle
member.summary: ECABottle is a component that represents a virtual bottle capable of containing and dispensing liquid within the environment. It maintains internal state variables such as capOpen and flipped, and interacts with objects equipped with an component to manage liquid flow and spawning. The bottle provides actions for flipping, opening or closing the cap, and starting or stopping the liquid flow. Several default automation rules are initialized at startup: - Flipping the bottle downward while the cap is open causes liquid to flow. - Flipping the bottle upward stops the liquid flow. - Closing the cap stops the liquid flow. - Opening the cap while the bottle is flipped downward resumes the liquid flow.

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.capOpen
member.summary: capOpen indicates whether the cap of the bottle is open (YES) or closed (NO).

member.name: P:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.flipped
member.summary: flipped indicates whether the bottle is currently flipped upside down (YES) or upright (NO).

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle._OpenCap(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: opens-cap represents the action of opening the cap of a bottle equipped with an component. When executed, it sets the internal state variable capOpen to true and, if the bottle is flipped downward, triggers the drops-liquid action to start the liquid flow.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle._CloseCap(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: closes-cap represents the action of closing the cap of a bottle equipped with an component. When executed, it sets the internal state variable capOpen to false and triggers the stops-dropping action to halt any ongoing liquid flow.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.FlipsDown
member.summary: flips-down represents the action of turning a bottle equipped with an component upside down. When executed, it updates the internal state variable flipped to true and, if the cap is open, triggers the drops-liquid action to start the liquid flow.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.FlipsUp
member.summary: flips-up represents the action of turning a bottle equipped with an component to an upright position. When executed, it updates the internal state variable flipped to false and stops any ongoing liquid flow if the bottle was previously pouring.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.DropsLiquid
member.summary: drops liquid represents the action that initiates the release of liquid from the spawner associated with an object equipped with an component. When executed, it starts the spawning of liquid particles or drops within the environment.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.StopsDropping
member.summary: stops dropping represents the internal action that stops the flow of liquid from an object equipped with an component. When executed, it halts the spawning of liquid particles or drops and updates the dispenser’s internal state to indicate that the liquid flow has stopped.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECABottle.ToggleCap
member.summary: Toggles the cap state between open and closed, and publishes the appropriate action.

member.name: T:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap
member.summary: ECAWaterMixerTap is a component that represents a virtual water mixer tap within the environment. It can be turned to the left, idle (center), or right positions, corresponding respectively to warm, neutral, and cold water flow states. Several default automation rules are initialized at startup: - Turning left starts the flow of warm water. - Turning right starts the flow of cold water. - Returning to the idle (center) position stops the water flow. The component provides properties and actions for controlling its rotation state, managing the water flow, and responding to user interactions or automation triggers.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap.FlowsWarmWater
member.summary: flows-warm-water represents the action that causes an object equipped with an component, typically a tap, to emit warm water.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap.FlowsColdWater
member.summary: FlowsColdWater causes the tap to emit cold water.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap.StopFlowingWater
member.summary: StopFlowingWater stops any water from flowing.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap._TurnsLeft(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: TurnsLeft is an action where a character turns the tap handle to the left.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap._TurnsIdle(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: TurnsIdle is an action where a character returns the tap handle to the center (idle) position.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Props.Subcategories.LiquidDispenser.Subcategories.ECAWaterMixerTap._TurnsRight(ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Characters.ECACharacter)
member.summary: TurnsRight is an action where a character turns the tap handle to the right.

member.summary: ECASystem is a component that represents the virtual automation system within the environment. It handles system-level initialization events and notifies when the virtual system starts, enabling the execution of ECA rules and actions defined to occur at application startup.

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Taxonomies.Objects.Systems.ECASystem.StartsUpTest
member.summary: starts up represents the action that triggers the startup process of the virtual system. When executed, it signals that the system has initialized successfully and can serve as a trigger for ECA automation rules that should run at application startup.

member.example: Per il verbo 'changes', la lista (value del dizionario) avrà due valori

Path del file audio richiesto

member.name: M:ECARules4All_DLL.Utils.TaxonomyUtils.AgileErrorStatus(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Stampa un messaggio di errore esaustivo nella console. Utile per capire l'origine dei problemi su WebGL.
member.remarks:
E' possibile usare dei codici per visualizzare log di errore (e), warning (w) o debug (l).
(e) PARAMETERS_ERROR: "La funzione richiede funData parametri. Oggetto che l'ha richiesta: objName"
(e) OBJECT_NOT_FOUND: "L'oggetto chiamato objName non esiste"
(e) ILLEGAL_CHARACTERS: "Impossibile rinominare l'oggetto objName in funData. L'oggetto presenta dei caratteri illegali."
(e) NAME_ALREADY_USED: "Impossibile rinominare l'oggetto objName in funData. Nome già utilizzato."
(e) PROPERTY_NOT_EXISTING: "L'oggetto objName non dispone della proprietà funData"
(e) PROPERTY_CONFLICTING: "Impossibile applicare la proprietà funData.Split(AgileSep).First(). L'oggetto objName dispone della proprietà funData.Split(AgileSep).Last() che va in conflitto con quella richiesta."
(e) ILLEGAL_NAME: "Si è verificato un errore, riprova. Se il problema persiste, consulta le istruzioni del software con il seguente codice errore: error-08"
(e) CONNECTION_ERROR: "Si è verificato un problema nella richiesta HTTP per il file funData. Oggetto che ha richiesto la risorsa: objName"
(e) CRITICAL_ERROR: "Si è verificato un errore critico. Se il problema persiste, si consiglia di riavviare il software.\n Dettagli errore: funData"
(e) INVALID_FORMAT: "Formato della stringa non valido. Dettagli errore: funData"
(e) NOT_ECA_OBJECT: "Impossibile applicare la regola all'oggetto objName. L'oggetto non dispone di una proprietà per gestirla."
(e) ECA_RULE_NOT_VALID: "La regola non è valida. Oggetto che l'ha respinta: objName. Maggiori dettagli: funData"
(w) GENERIC_WARNING: "Warning generico. Dettagli: funData"
(w) IS_COROUTINE: "In attesa del termine della coroutine. Maggiori dettagli: funData"
(w) SOURCE_WARNING: "Il parametro source non può essere nullo o vuoto!"
(l) LOG: funData

members.member:
